LAW AND MOTION 301, NO APPEARANCES ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANTS MINH HO AND DAISY THAI'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED. THERE REMAINS TRIABLE ISSUES WHETHER PLAINTIFFS EFFECTIVELY AMENDED THE TERMS OF DEFENDANTS TENANCY SUCH THAT THE 3 DAY NOTICE STATED A PROPER BASIS FOR EVICTION. MINI MINUTES: Dept. 514 Judge: Ellen Chaitin; Clerk: S. Ravinantapricha; Not Reported.

Brenda Cruz Keith, Esq. for Plaintiff Wayne Dong.

Jessica Alexandra, Esq., for Defendant Minh Ho.

The Judge Pro Tem: Gary Louie, litigants and counsel confer privately in Dept. 504B, for settlement discussions.
Case DID NOT settle, the trial date confirmed.

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Feb-08-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

NO APPEARANCES ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS:
DEFENDANTS MINH HO AND DAISY THAI'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED. THERE REMAINS TRIABLE ISSUES WHETHER PLAINTIFFS EFFECTIVELY AMENDED THE TERMS OF DEFENDANTS TENANCY SUCH THAT THE 3 DAY NOTICE STATED A PROPER BASIS FOR EVICTION. Opposing party is to submit a proposed form of order pursuant to CCP 437 C(G). Judge: Peter J. Busch, Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR # 8189 =301/PJB
